design_philosophy.core_loop: Race toy vehicles around household environments, maintaining track position and competing against opponents to qualify for next round or achieve screen dominance in head-to-head mode
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Positional racing with forced single-screen viewport creating spatial pressure; multiplayer tension between pursuing screen edge dominance vs. traditional first-place finishing
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot miniaturized toy vehicles (formula one cars, tanks, powerboats, helicopters) through everyday household obstacle courses
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Rejects split-screen convention entirely; instead uses single-screen multiplayer where trailing player becomes invisible once leader gains sufficient distance, creating asymmetric win conditions. Two distinct game modes with different victory mechanics (position-based vs. visibility-based). Creative use of household environments as racing venues rather than traditional tracks

difficulty.lives_system: Three chances (races) in Micro Machines Challenge mode; losing three times ends game. Every three races completes a time trial to earn an extra chance
difficulty.checkpointing: Players must finish first or second to advance per round; failure to place grants another attempt (three total attempts allowed before game over)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Head-to-head multiplayer mode includes character handicaps to balance skill differences between players. Two distinct modes allow players to choose position-based or visibility-based competition

progression.unlock_mechanism: Advance through Micro Machines Challenge by placing first or second each race; progress gates behind three-race win requirement and periodic time trials
replayability_hooks: Head-to-head multiplayer mode with different victory condition (visibility/screen dominance vs. position)
Multiple playable characters with varying handicaps
Variety of household environment tracks (breakfast tables, desks, treerooms, pool tables, etc.)
Two-player modes including Game Gear link-up support and split-controller single-console play

world_building_techniques: Environmental theming around household spaces (breakfast tables, work desks, treehouses, pool tables)
Toy vehicle aesthetics establishing Micro Machines toy brand connection
Themed obstacles matching environment (cereal boxes, pencil sharpeners)

environmental_storytelling: Household environments communicate scale (toy-sized vehicles in human-scale spaces)
Vehicle types themed to environment (tanks in battle-themed spaces, powerboats in pool areas, helicopters in open areas)
Obstacle placement creates implicit narrative of navigating everyday hazards
